column1: Plant in wet soil: Sweet flag thrives in wet or waterlogged soil
column2: so choose a planting location with good moisture retention.

column1: Provide full sun to part shade: Sweet flag prefers full sun but can tolerate partial shade
column2: so ensure it receives adequate sunlight for healthy growth.

column1: Keep soil consistently moist: Maintain a consistently moist environment around the plant to mimic its natural habitat
Space plants 30 cm apart: When planting multiple sweet flag plants
column2: space them about 30 centimetes apart to allow room for their growth.

column1: Mulch to retain moisture: Apply mulch around the base of the plant to help retain soil moisture and protect the root system.
Fertilize in spring: Apply a balanced
column2: slow-release fertilizer in the spring to provide essential nutrients for the growing season.

column1: Prune dead leaves: Remove any dead or damaged leaves to promote a tidy appearance and prevent disease.
Divide every 2-3 years: Divide mature sweet flag plants every 2-3 years to rejuvenate them and maintain vigorous growth.
Control pests organically: Use organic methods like neem oil or beneficial insects to manage common pests without harmful chemicals.
Harvest rhizomes in fall: If you wish to propagate sweet flag
column2: harvest rhizomes in the fall and replant or store them for future use.
